stem: A rectangular park is 120 meters long and 50 meters wide. A jogger wants to take a shortcut by running diagonally from one corner of the park to the opposite corner. What is the length, in meters, of the diagonal path?
explanation: The diagonal of the rectangle forms the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 120 m and 50 m. Using the Pythagorean theorem: d = √(120² + 50²) = √(14400 + 2500) = √16900 = 130 meters. This is a 5-12-13 Pythagorean triple scaled by 10.

distractorRationale.A: Added the two side lengths instead of using the Pythagorean theorem, computing 120 + 50 = 170.
distractorRationale.C: Averaged the two side lengths instead of using the Pythagorean theorem, computing (120 + 50) ÷ 2 = 85.
distractorRationale.D: Subtracted the two side lengths instead of using the Pythagorean theorem, computing 120 - 50 = 70.
